title: Broadcom Symantec SEP Application and Device Control Policy Violations
dataSources: `sourcetype=symantec:ep:security:file` events for Application Control and Device Control (USB, peripheral) policy breaches
app: Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection (Splunkbase 2772)
spl:
index=endpoint sourcetype="symantec:ep:security:file" earliest=-7d
| eval host=coalesce(Computer_Name, computer_name, host)
| eval desc=coalesce(Event_Description, event_description, _raw)
| where match(lower(desc), "(?i)application control|device control|usb|removable|blocked by policy|peripheral")
| eval policy=coalesce(Policy_Name, policy_name, "unknown")
| stats count by host policy desc
| sort -count
description: Application and Device Control policies stop unapproved software and removable media from exfiltrating data or running untrusted binaries. Aggregating policy violations reveals users or systems routinely fighting controls and highlights policy gaps.
value: Strengthens data-loss prevention at the endpoint and gives auditors evidence that technical enforcement of software and USB restrictions is operating.
implementation: (1) Ensure SEPM logs Application and Device Control events. (2) Normalize `Policy_Name` and descriptions. (3) Group by business unit via CMDB. (4) Separate expected test machines with a lookup. (5) Weekly review with desktop engineering for top violating hosts.
visualization: Bar chart (violations by policy), table (host × policy × count), pie (block vs audit-only if action field exists).

references.title: Documentation — Symantec Endpoint Protection Application Control
Splunkbase — Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection

grandmaExplanation: We track when Broadcom Symantec blocks unapproved programs or USB devices so risky software and thumb drives cannot spread malware or steal data.
